hints.to eat: That would be 'edere/esse' (different verb) — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to go: That would be 'ire' — 'est' is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to stand: That would be 'stare' — 'est' is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive of 'esse' would be 'sit' — 'est' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuit' — 'est' is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect of 'esse' would be 'erat' — 'est' is present indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat), which also has infinitive 'esse'
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become/be made)

hints.accusative: While 'semen' looks identical in accusative, here it is the subject — nominative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'semine' — 'Semen' is nominative subject
hints.vocative: Vocative would be used for direct address — 'Semen' is nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'seminis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'seminis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'seminis' ends in -is

hints.accusative: While 'verbum' is identical in accusative, with 'est' it serves as predicate nominative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'verbo' — 'verbum' is predicate nominative with 'est'
hints.vocative: Vocative is used for direct address — 'verbum' is predicate nominative here

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Dei' is genitive singular
hints.dative: Dative would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' is genitive, showing possession
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'Deus' or 'Dee' — 'Dei' is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Dei' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Dei' ends in -i

translation: The seed is the word of God
